$comment: This file is generated via firehose. DO NOT edit by hand. Make any changes required using the firehose repo instead.
title: measurementAltitude
description: Height above a user-specified reference frame.

timeOfValidity.description: time_of_validity: Time at which the measurement is considered to be valid.

oneOf.description: AGL: height above ground level (AGL). Generally, position and terrain elevation data are required to convert between AGL and (MSL or HAE).
MSL: height above mean sea level (MSL). Generally, position is required to convert between MSL and HAE.
HAE: height above the WGS-84 ellipsoid (HAE), also known as geodetic altitude.

altitude.description: altitude: Altitude in meters, with altitude defined by the enum, altitude_type.  Units: m

errorModel.description: error_model (enum): Defines an optional error model for other than zero-mean, additive, white Gaussian noise (AWGN).

oneOf.description: NONE: No additional error model provided (num_error_model_params = 0).

FOGM: First-order Gauss-Markov (FOGM) process, defined by the following parameters (num_error_model_params = 3):   1) standard_deviation, units: m, description: standard deviation of a scalar, first order   Gauss-Markov measurement noise model.   2) time_constant, units: seconds, description: time constant of a scalar, first order   Gauss-Markov measurement noise model.   3) initial_standard_deviation, units: m, description: initial standard deviation of a   scalar, first order Gauss-Markov measurement noise model.

numErrorModelParams.description: num_error_model_params: Number of parameters required for the error model chosen.

errorModelParams.description: error_model_params: Error model parameters that characterize the optional error model. Units: various

integrity.description: integrity: Measurement integrity.  Includes the integrity method used and an integrity value (which is to be interpreted based upon the integrity method).  The intent of allowing num_integrity > 1 is to report multiple integrity values based on multiple integrity methods.
